title: shattering smoky croatian peanuts bowl
description: a cozy, smoky dish featuring peanuts in a stone bowl, inspired by the flavors of croatia. ayurvedic-friendly, rajasic-friendly, and perfect as a snack or dessert.
instructions: preheat a dolsot stone bowl in the oven to 400f (200c) for 30 minutes.
add 2 tablespoons of peanut oil to the preheated stone bowl and swirl to coat the sides.
in a separate pan, toast 1 tsp of smoked paprika over low heat for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
add the toasted smoked paprika to the peanut oil in the stone bowl and mix to combine.
add 1/2 cup of roasted peanuts to the stone bowl and mix to combine with the peanut oil and smoked paprika.
sprinkle 1 tsp of gosho salt and 1/4 tsp of black pepper over the peanuts and mix to combine.
add 1 tsp of honey and 1 tsp of ghee to the stone bowl and mix to combine.
sprinkle 1/4 tsp of crushed caraway seeds and 1/4 tsp of ayurvedic turmeric over the top of the peanuts.
sprinkle 1/4 cup of crushed fresh cilantro over the top of the peanuts.
place the dolsot stone bowl under the broiler for 1-2 minutes, until the peanuts are fragrant and the oil is bubbling.
remove the stone bowl from the oven and let cool for 1 minute.
serve the shattering smoky croatian peanuts bowl immediately, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
note: the peanuts will shatter when broken apart with a spoon, releasing a fragrant, smoky aroma.
